buggy_code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
row_means = np.mean(arr, axis=0)
task: Compute the mean of each row in a 2-D array
clarification_query: Should the mean be computed along rows (giving one value per row) or along columns (giving one value per column)?
clarification_options: along rows — one mean per row (axis=1)
along columns — one mean per column (axis=0)
expected_user_response: along rows — one mean per row (axis=1)
corrected_code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
row_means = np.mean(arr, axis=1)
explanation: axis=0 reduces along the first dimension (columns), producing one mean per column. To get one mean per row the correct argument is axis=1.
